senses.definition: A surname of notable individuals, especially a family name associated with the German-born Polish anatomist Johannes Purkinje, or with the Purkinje cells in the brain.
examples: Purkinje cells were named after Jan Evangelista Purkinje.
The Purkinje family has several notable scientists.

senses.definition: Relating to Purkinje, especially Purkinje cells in the cerebellum.
examples: Purkinjean cells showed distinctive branching patterns.
Purkinjean tissue samples were studied under the microscope.

senses.definition: A close-fitting knitted fabric stitch that creates a raised line; also the edge or seam on knitted fabric.
examples: She stitched a neat purl along the scarf.
The sweater had prominent purls along the cuffs.

senses.definition: To form a purl in knitting; to work a purl stitch.
examples: She purls the next row to create texture.
Purling alternates with knitting to form ribbing.

senses.definition: Having a raised knitted stitch; decorated with purl stitches.
examples: The purled border gave the scarf texture.
A purled pattern runs along the edge.

senses.definition: A person who purls (knits purl stitches); a knitter who uses purl stitches.
examples: The purler carefully formed each purl stitch.
A talented purler created a textured scarf.

senses.definition: People who purl; plural of purler, those who make purl stitches in knitting.
examples: The purlers shared techniques at the workshop.
Several purlers demonstrated ribbing with purl stitches.

senses.definition: A house or shop where knitting, especially purl crafting, is practiced or sold; a historic term for a knitters' shop.
examples: The purlhouse displayed handmade scarves.
She visited the purlhouse to buy yarn.

senses.definition: An old or humorous term for the thumb or the space at the base of the thumb; used in some dialects or historical texts.
examples: The old manuscript mentions the purlicue in playful language.
He counted on his purlicue for grip.

senses.definition: Possessing a purlicue; having a prominent thumb area.
examples: Purlicued hands aided the craftsman.
A purlicued grip helped hold the paddle.

senses.definition: Multiple purlicues; the spaces at the base of thumbs in several hands.
examples: Her purlicues showed calluses from rowing.
The monk described purlicues in the old text.

senses.definition: Acting with the purlicue or using the purlicue area; handling with the thumb.
examples: He purlicuing along the rope gave him leverage.
The craftsman was purlicuing the tool carefully.

senses.definition: A place or area that one visits frequently; a secondary or adjoining area; a polite term for a place to go; sometimes meaning a neighborhood or outskirts.
examples: The market was within his purlieu of daily walks.
They explored the riverbank, a familiar purlieu of theirs.

senses.definition: A Scots or Northern English term for a resident of a purlieu; a person who frequents a particular area.
examples: The purlieuman patrolled the outskirts.
Locals called him a purlieuman for his daily visits.

senses.definition: Plural form of purlieuman; multiple residents or frequent visitors of a purlieu.
examples: The purlieumen gathered at the gate.
Stories circulated among the purlieumen.

senses.definition: Areas near a place, especially surrounding districts or outskirts; roaming grounds or neighborhoods.
examples: The purlieus around the old mill were quiet at night.
Neighborhood purlieus often had shared markets.

senses.definition: An English spelling variant of purlieu; a place near or surrounding a main area.
examples: They ventured into the purlieux of the town.
The old map marks the purlieux clearly.

senses.definition: A horizontal beam along the length of a building, supporting rafters; a structural timber.
examples: The roof was repaired with new purlins.
Purlins run along the length of the shed.

senses.definition: Alternative or obsolete spelling of purlin; a horizontal beam in a roof.
examples: The purline needed replacement.
Old carpenters spoke of the purline with reverence.

senses.definition: Plural of purlin; multiple horizontal roof beams.
examples: The roof was supported by several purlines.
Measure the distance between purlines.

senses.definition: The act of working a purl stitch in knitting.
examples: She is purling on the second row.
Purling creates a ribbed texture.

senses.definition: Instances of the act of purling; multiple purl stitches or sessions of purling.
examples: Her purlings produced a neat texture.
Several purlings were needed for the ribbed edge.

senses.definition: Alternate spelling of purlin; roof beams.
examples: Replace the damaged purlins before winter.
The attic rafters rested on the purlins.

senses.definition: A person who arranges or works with purls; a craftsman dealing with knitting or beams (historical/rarer usage).
examples: The old trade lists mention a purlman.

senses.definition: To steal something; to take dishonestly.
examples: He purloined the neighbor's bicycle.
They purloined funds from the petty till.

senses.definition: Having been stolen; taken dishonestly.
examples: He noticed purloined items missing from the desk.
The purloined letters were recovered.
